$comment: c42 D-CSUBSET-INDEX-NEGATIVE-WIDEN + c93 D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA (C 6.5.2.1): a NEGATIVE subscript p[-n] must step -n*sizeof(*p) bytes. c42 (RUNTIME/DEBUG): a 32-bit index reached the 64-bit lea/ADD un-widened, so a negative offset zero-extended to a +4GiB address -> SIGSEGV; lowerGep sign-extends the runtime index. c93 (CONSTANT/RELEASE): under ConstFold a constant p[-N] folds to a bare NEGATIVE Const byte-offset -> the const-disp Gep -> `lea [base + MemOffset(<0)]`; x86 encodes a hardware-sign-extended `lea [base+disp32]` (one instruction) but arm64's every base+disp lea/ADD-imm variant is UNSIGNED-magnitude-bounded, so a negative disp matched NO variant (A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant opcode 'lea' width 64) and the function was dropped. c93's config-gated fold (targetHasSignedDispLea() false on arm64) materializes the negative disp into a fresh GPR and emits the base+index ADD Xd,Xn,Xm form (Xm = sign-extended -N = base - N) -- the SAME mechanism the runtime-subscript path uses. So this corpus now declares the release + full-release-like arms (previously omitted precisely to dodge this arm64 gap) and is GREEN on all 4 targets at BOTH pipeline levels. Strides 1 (char*), 4 (int*), 12 (struct, non-pow2), each constant + runtime, PLUS constant-negative-index STOREs (int + char) round-tripping the written bytes. Exit 42 iff all 12 sub-cases pass. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the c42 widen -> crash (non-42) on the first runtime-Mul subscript in debug; revert the c93 fold -> the arm64 release arm fails to compile (A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant lea width 64) on the first constant p[-N] (x86 stays green via signed disp32). sqlite uses p[-1] lookback -> run-green-critical.
